title: Antillanthus trichotomus
intro: Antillanthus trichotomus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the 'Puerto Rican copperleaf.' The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being. Antillanthus trichotomus contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are popular remedies in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.
